Community AMA with Erica Louie (dbt Labs)
Network event
D

Hosted by dbt L.

Super Organizer

Halifax dbt Meetup
1 review
Details

REGISTER HERE 👉 https://www.getdbt.com/resources/webinars/community-slack-ama

🚀 Hey Community! Are you ready to dive deep, get insights, and satisfy your curiosity?

We’re hosting monthly live Ask Me Anything events! It's the perfect platform to quench your curiosity and get to know each other better. Don't miss out on this opportunity to connect and learn together.

What to Expect:

Discussing trends in the analytics space
Sharing key takeaways from the recent events
Hearing your questions and thoughts

Speaker: Erica Louie, Data @ dbt Labs
Host: Bolaji Oyejide, Digital Community Mgr @ dbt Labs
Topic: Careers in data, building a data team, the dbt Semantic Layer, and scaling your dbt project
When: November 21st @ 2pm EST
Where: Virtual and in #dbt-community-merge

Erica was the second employee at dbt Labs, then called Fishtown Analytics and she's seen dbt grow, a lot. In this AMA, Erica will share more on her career in data, her strong opinions on building a data team, how she manages her team, the dbt Semantic Layer, scaling your dbt project, and more on her Substack.

Register now to get the link to watch and join the #dbt-community-ama channel in Slack to participate live!

Read more
Speaker
Erica Louie, dbt Labs

Second hire at dbt Labs, former Head of Data, and now leading the product data team (and previously worked for BuzzFeed's data team), Erica "ric" Louie proudly hails from a liberal arts background (studied political science + italian) and grew her career in data at dbt Labs starting from the ground-up. She firmly believes that anyone can be a data practitioner with the right mentors, resources (sans trivial data jargon), and a good sense of humor. While rethinking the philosophy behind data systems, operations, and teams is one of her favorite topics, she enjoys thinking and writing about the human experience, and how it intersects with the work we choose to do and who we choose to do it with.

Halifax dbt Meetup Q4

Hosted by Saadat Q. and 2 others

dbt L. is a Super Organizer

Halifax dbt Meetup
1 review
Details

A dbt Meetup is a networking event open to all folks working with data! Talks predominantly focus on community members' experience with dbt, however, you'll catch presentations on broader topics such as analytics engineering, data stacks, data ops, modeling, testing, and team structures. Data practionners, amateur hackers & students are all welcome!

🤝Organizer: Esther Fraser & Saadat Qadri
🏠Venue Host: CoWork Halifax

To attend, please read the Health and Safety Policy and Terms of Participation: https://www.getdbt.com/legal/health-and-safety-policy

📝Agenda

12h00 - 12h15 | Welcome & Introductions
12h15 - 12h45| Data on Autopilot: An Overview of Devops with dbt by Adam Duval
12h45 - 13h30 | Open Workshop
13h30 - 13h55 | Networking & Snacking
13h55 - 14h00 | Closing Remarks

Data on Autopilot: An Overview of Devops with dbt
Explore how DevOps, particularly CI/CD, pipelines with dbt can enhance your data workflows. By automating data transformations, testing, and deployments, organizations can achieve faster, more reliable insights while minimizing manual errors. This discussion will highlight the importance of integrating DevOps practices into analytics engineering. It will cover the fundamental concepts of DevOps and CI/CD with dbt, before delving into some more advanced workflow examples and finishing with the latest advanced CI features announced at Coalesce 2024. The goal is to empower attendees to streamline data processes and make their pipelines work for them, on autopilot.

Open Workshop
After the success exploring unit tests in last quarter's Open Workshop segment, we're making it a permanent feature! Got a thorny dbt problem that giving you some trouble? Or did you just solve one and want to brag? Bring your laptop and let your community lend a hand.

➡️ Join the dbt Slack community: https://www.getdbt.com/community/
🤝For the best Meetup experience, make sure to join the #local-eastern-canada channel in dbt Slack (https://slack.getdbt.com/).
----------------------------------

dbt is the standard in data transformation, used by over 30,000 organizations worldwide. Through the application of software engineering best practices like modularity, version control, testing, and documentation, dbt’s analytics engineering workflow helps teams work more efficiently to produce data the entire organization can trust. Learn more: https://www.getdbt.com/

Read more
Speaker
Adam Duval

Adam is  a data analytics professional with 15+ years of experience helping organizations turn data challenges into actionable insights. In his current role as an Analytics Engineer, he develops end-to-end analytics solutions to help business units achieve strategic goals. In previous roles, he has been responsible for implementing analytics stacks to modernize customer value-added technical deliverables and rebuilding key reporting pipelines focusing on data quality and uptime. His goal is always to transform data into action.

Halifax dbt Meetup
1 review
Details

What are dbt meetups? dbt Meetups are networking events open to all folks working with data! Talks predominantly focus on community members' experience with dbt, however, you'll catch presentations on broader topics such as analytics engineering, data stacks, data ops, modeling, testing, and team structures. Data practionners, amateur hackers & students are all welcome!

🤝Organizer: Esther Fraser
🏠Venue Host: CoWork Halifax

To attend, please read the Health and Safety Policy and Terms of Participation: https://www.getdbt.com/legal/health-and-safety-policy

📝Agenda

12h00 - 12h15 | Welcome & Introductions
12h15 - 12h45| Presentation: Canonical Models with Saadat Qadri
12h45 - 13h30 | Open Workshop
13h30 - 13h55 | Networking & Snacking
13h55 - 14h00 | Closing Remarks

Presentation: Canonical Models - The Missing Piece of the Analytics Development Life CycleIn this talk, I’ll explore the concept of a “canonical data model”, and how these models help enhance data consistency and quality. Canonical models serve as standardized frameworks, ensuring a unified understanding of data across an organization, much like GAAP does for financial reporting. By reducing repetitive design efforts, these models can help streamline the data modeling process, allowing for efficient reuse and significant time savings. They also facilitate better collaboration among analytics engineers by providing a common language for data interpretation. A third benefit is Improved data quality, as canonical models can help enforce data integrity and validation in source systems. Through a couple of examples, I’ll illustrate how canonical models bring consistency and shared definitions across diverse data producers and consumers, ultimately leading to more reliable analytics and better decision-making.

Open Workshop
This quarter, we're trying out an Open Workshop segment. Got a thorny dbt problem that giving you some trouble? Or did you just solve one and want to brag? Bring your laptop and let your community lend a hand.

➡️ Join the dbt Slack community: https://www.getdbt.com/community/
🤝For the best Meetup experience, make sure to join the #local-eastern-canada channel in dbt Slack (https://slack.getdbt.com/).
----------------------------------

dbt is the standard in data transformation, used by over 30,000 organizations worldwide. Through the application of software engineering best practices like modularity, version control, testing, and documentation, dbt’s analytics engineering workflow helps teams work more efficiently to produce data the entire organization can trust. Learn more: https://www.getdbt.com/

Read more
Speaker
Saadat Qadri

Saadat Qadri is an analytics professional with over 15 years of experience designing, implementing, and supporting enterprise data strategies that drive business outcomes. He started his data career as an analyst at Freshbooks and was most recently VP of Data at Karbon, where he led the data team and spearheaded the development of Karbon Practice Intelligence, an award-winning analytics product for accounting firms. Through Modern (modern.inc), Saadat specializes in establishing data infrastructure for revenue intelligence, helping businesses unlock the full potential of their data.

Saadat lives with his wife and two children in Halifax. When not writing SQL, he's usually trying to work on his pour-over skills.

🚀 Hey Community! Are you ready to dive deep, get insights, and satisfy your curiosity?

We’re hosting monthly live Ask Me Anything events! It's the perfect platform to quench your curiosity and get to know each other better. Don't miss out on this opportunity to connect and learn together.

What to Expect:

Discussing trends in the analytics space
Sharing key takeaways from the recent events
Hearing your questions and thoughts

Speaker: Alex Welch, Head of Data @dbt Labs
Topic: BI Tools & Semantic Layer
When: June 27 @ 4pm EST
Where: Virtual and in #dbt-community-merge

Whether you’re looking at flight data, sales figures, consumer transactions, or biometric stats — access to the correct sources + a well-trained user + BI tools + dbt = data that works!!
This month we have Alex Welch, Head of Data at dbt Labs, to answer questions and discuss more about BI tools, analytics trends & the Semantic Layer!

Register now to get the link to watch and join the #dbt-community-merge channel in Slack to participate live!

Halifax dbt meetup Q2

Hosted by Esther F.

Halifax dbt Meetup
1 review
Details

This dbt Meetup is an opportunity for Halifax data folks to connect and collaborate. If you work with data, this event is for you! We welcome data analysts, scientists, engineers, architects, and more. Professionals, students and amateur hackers welcome!

Pizza will be served (including gluten-free and vegetarian options) and you're welcome to bring your own lunch!

12h00 Welcome & ice breakers
12h15 Presentation — Michelle Forrest (Associate Director of Data Solutions, Brooklyn Data Co) on Data Governance
12h45 Breakout Discussion
13h00 Mix & Mingle

To attend, please read the Health and Safety Policy and Terms of Participation: https://www.getdbt.com/legal/health-and-safety-policy

----------------------------------
dbt is a data transformation framework that lets analysts and engineers collaborate using their shared knowledge of SQL. Through the application of software engineering best practices like modularity, version control, testing, and documentation, dbt’s analytics engineering workflow helps teams work more efficiently to produce data the entire organization can trust.

Learn more: https://www.getdbt.com/

➡️ Join the dbt Slack community: https://community.getdbt.com/
🤝 Be part of the conversation in the #local-eastern-canada channel in dbt Slack! This is where the chat will also take place during meetups.

Read more
Speaker
Michelle Forrest

Michelle Forrest is an Associate Director of Data Solutions for Brooklyn Data, a Velir consultancy company that partners with its clients to build data capabilities. Prior to that Michelle was the Head of Data at Wattpad, a leading social storytelling platform out of Toronto with worldwide users.

With a background in Computer Science and Project Management certification, Michelle has always focused on Technology and for the past several years in particular on the data space. She has worked in both professional services and product companies across several domains in both Canada and the United Arab Emirates.
In her downtime when she isn't playing sports or enjoying NS lake life she is traveling to new destinations.
